stem: Find the length of the arc of the curve \( y = \dfrac{x^{3}}{[[t6]]} + \dfrac{[[t]]}{2x} \) from \( x = [[a]] \) to \( x = [[b]] \).
solution: Here \( y' = \dfrac{x^{2}}{[[t2]]} - \dfrac{[[t]]}{2x^{2}} \), so \( 1+(y')^{2} = \left(\dfrac{x^{2}}{[[t2]]} + \dfrac{[[t]]}{2x^{2}}\right)^{2} \) is a perfect square. Thus the arc length is \( \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]}\left(\dfrac{x^{2}}{[[t2]]} + \dfrac{[[t]]}{2x^{2}}\right)dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This evaluates \( y([[b]])-y([[a]]) \) (the change in the function), not the arc length.
This keeps only the \( \dfrac{x^{2}}{[[t2]]} \) term of the integrand, dropping \( \dfrac{[[t]]}{2x^{2}} \).
This doubles the correct arc length.
This is off by one from the correct arc length.
This is off by one from the correct arc length.

stem: Find the length of the arc of the curve \( y = \cosh(x) \) from \( x = [[a]] \) to \( x = [[b]] \).
solution: Here \( y' = \sinh(x) \), so \( 1+(y')^{2} = 1+\sinh^{2}(x) = \cosh^{2}(x) \) is a perfect square (a hyperbolic identity), and \( \sqrt{1+(y')^{2}} = \cosh(x) \) since \( \cosh(x)\ge 0 \). Thus the arc length is \( \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]}\cosh(x)\,dx = \sinh([[b]]) - \sinh([[a]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This evaluates \( y([[b]])-y([[a]]) \) (the change in the function), not the arc length.
This adds the endpoint values of \( \sinh \) instead of subtracting them.
This is the length of a straight chord, ignoring the curve's curvature entirely.
This has the wrong sign, evaluating the endpoints in reverse order.
This is off by one from the correct arc length.

stem: Find the length of the arc of the curve \( y = \dfrac{1}{6}\left((x-[[h]])^{2}+4\right)^{3/2} \) from \( x = [[a]] \) to \( x = [[b]] \).
solution: Here \( y' = \dfrac{x-[[h]]}{2}\sqrt{(x-[[h]])^{2}+4} \), so \( 1+(y')^{2} = \left(\dfrac{(x-[[h]])^{2}}{2}+1\right)^{2} \) is a perfect square, and \( \sqrt{1+(y')^{2}} = \dfrac{(x-[[h]])^{2}}{2}+1 \) (always positive). Thus the arc length is \( \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]}\left(\dfrac{(x-[[h]])^{2}}{2}+1\right)dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This evaluates \( y([[b]])-y([[a]]) \) (the change in the function), not the arc length.
This drops the \( +1 \) term inside the perfect-square integrand.
This is the length of a straight chord, ignoring the curve's curvature entirely.
This doubles the correct arc length.
This is off by one from the correct arc length.
